title: willoughbya houstoniana
intro: Willoughbya houstoniana, also known as the resurrection fern, is a type of epiphytic fern native to the southeastern United States, known for its unique ability to survive long periods of drought by drying out and appearing dead, only to revive when moisture is restored. The medicinal properties of Willoughbya houstoniana include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of the plant include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory problems, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of Willoughbya houstoniana are typically made by infusing the dried fronds in hot water, and can be consumed as a tea or used topically to treat wounds and skin irritations.
